name: Jellyfink
description: Connect to remote Jellyfin servers and present their libraries locally without copying media.
overview: Remote Jellyfin library sharing - a metadata and playback bridge.

versions.changelog: Jellyfin 10.11 support (now requires 10.11+/net9). Fixes the TypeLoad/MissingMethod crashes on 10.11 (moved User type, SkiaSharp 3, changed task-trigger and user APIs). Validated on 10.11.11: plugin loads, user dropdown works, sync runs, REMOTE library badge applies. Also includes the dedicated jellyfink.log.

Fix Jellyfin 10.11 compatibility (IUserManager.Users MissingMethodException that broke the user dropdown and watch-state/favorites). Add a dedicated jellyfink.log file in Jellyfin's log directory for the plugin's own events (sync results, failures, stream audit, notifications); path shown in the status panel. The library-badge step now logs whether it applied or why it skipped.

Content filters (year/rating/genre), quiet-hours scheduling, failure/offline webhook notifications, per-run sync report, stream audit log, auto Direct->Proxy fallback, two-way favorites, per-server bitrate cap, library item-count preview, and the REMOTE library badge image. Includes a security review (no issues; webhook restricted to http(s)).

Add a real-time remote-server health check: each configured server shows online/offline (with version or 'unreachable'/'timed out') on the config page, refreshed on load and after save. New GET /Jellyfink/Health endpoint.

Round-2 polish (movies & TV only). Removed the music feature. Fixed: collection artwork now set; watch-state pull no longer clobbers newer local progress; season.nfo written; per-server 'max items' override; live-sync connection health shown in status; servers now sync concurrently; config-schema migration scaffold; quieter logs; added an automated test project.

Big feature + hardening pass (28 items): pull watch-state from remote; per-user mapping; music libraries; mirror collections; live WebSocket sync; targeted library scan; retry/backoff + HTTP timeouts; paginated fetch; configurable played threshold; signed-token TTL; rate-limited + validated stream endpoint; richer connection test; sync status panel; series-folder de-dup; parallel asset downloads; plugin icon.

Subtitles: expose the remote's embedded subtitle tracks so they can be picked like a direct connection (no sidecar download needed); sidecar download stays optional/off. Add a custom 'Library name prefix' (e.g. 'Remote ·') to group remote libraries together in the sidebar/home.

Config page: prominent warning not to leave Subtitle languages blank (blank pulls every language and can time out the remote), plus a save-time warning when subtitle download is on with no language filter.
Bug fixes: support remotes hosted under a subpath (relative request paths); unique per-server DeviceId to avoid token churn; proxy stream endpoint accepts HEAD requests.

Subtitles: hide broken embedded tracks (AllowEmbeddedSubtitles=None) and download working sidecar .srt resiliently (per-track timeout, language filter). Richer metadata: cast/crew, studios, ratings, premiere date, provider IDs, tagline, sort title, plus backdrops/fanart. Audit fixes: auth self-healing, reconcile marker guard, proxy disposal.

Bug-fix pass: auth self-healing on 401; reconcile data-loss guard (marker file); proxy stream disposal; fetch subtitle metadata only when enabled; manage-library-settings toggle; HTML escaping; cleanup.
Lean library options (no duplicate metadata/image fetch, no ffmpeg extraction); incremental sync; cleanup/prune; subtitle download off by default.
